title: SOFI Stock Slumps As Wall Street Slashes Price Targets
text: SoFi Technologies experienced significant decline, falling approximately 14.27%, sliding from near $20 in mid-April to around $15.74. TD Cowen reduced price target from $24 to $17, citing macro uncertainty, pressure on lower-income borrowers, and fierce consumer and auto lending competition. Keefe Bruyette cut targets from $20 to $17 with Underperform rating, warning of weakening credit metrics. Bank of America lowered target from $20 to $18, maintaining Underperform stance amid broader sector concerns. Muddy Waters issued short report claiming SoFi misaccounted a $312 million JPMorgan loan, potentially reversing approximately $1 billion in previously reported EBITDA. The company trades at premium P/E ratio of approximately 49 and price-to-sales ratio near 6.6 for consumer finance name, leaving limited room for earnings disappointment.
